item.title: We’re Going to Talk About the Future of Trading at Odd Lots Live
item.description: Come see us on May 28 in NYC.

item.title: Newsom Pitches Software Tax to Raise Billions in New Revenue
item.description: California Governor Gavin Newsom is proposing a new tax on cloud-based software sales to raise billions of dollars in state and local revenue in coming years.

item.title: Cerebras CEO Turns Year’s Largest IPO Into $3.2 Billion Fortune
item.description: Serial Silicon Valley entrepreneur Andrew Feldman, who literally grew up on the campus of Stanford University, was already involved in selling three companies and taking another public. But none of that compares with Thursday’s debut of his AI-chip manufacturer.

item.title: Argentina Inflation Slowed in April for First Time in 11 Months
item.description: Argentina’s inflation slowed for the first time in 11 months in a victory for President Javier Milei after prices had jumped on the Iran war-related oil shock in March.

item.title: Schonfeld-Backed Perbak Closing Hedge Fund After ‘Too-Short’ Run
item.description: Perbak Capital Partners, a UK-based hedge fund backed by Schonfeld Strategic Advisors, is winding down after determining its assets haven’t grown enough to maintain a “significant financial buffer” to protect the business.

item.title: Foreign Buyers Snap Up Nearly Half of Trump’s Emergency Oil
item.description: Nearly half of the crude oil released from the US Strategic Petroleum Reserve is being exported, a fresh sign of how severely global supplies have tightened amid the Iran war.

item.title: Benchmark, Eclipse Reap Billions in Returns From Cerebras IPO
item.description: Three of the first venture capital investors in Cerebras Systems Inc. are set to earn billions of dollars from their early bets on the chipmaker following its Wall Street debut on Thursday. Benchmark will own roughly 8.1% of the outstanding shares in Cerebras after the initial public offering, according to a securities filing. At the $185-per-share IPO price for Cerebras, that stake was worth about $3.2 billion. That marks a 12-fold return for Benchmark, which invested a total of $268 million in

item.title: Brazilian Oil Jobs Are at 16-Year High on Offshore Drilling Boom
item.description: Employment in the Brazilian oil industry has rebounded to the highest since the golden era of Petrobras’s mega-projects in 2010.

item.title: Crypto Billionaire Ehrsam Joins Risky Bet on Venezuela Revival
item.description: American crypto billionaire Fred Ehrsam has become one of the most visible, high-profile foreign investors courting Venezuela, betting the sanctions-hit nation could offer outsized returns as its battered economy reopens.

item.title: Car Salesman Says That Andrew Left’s Short Reports ‘Scared’ Him
item.description: The impact of short selling on every day investors was highlighted on Thursday at Citron Research founder Andrew Left’s criminal trial.

item.title: Peru Postpones Announcement of Election Results Until Sunday
item.description: Peru will delay the official announcement of first-round election results by two days as officials continue to resolve a record number of disputed tally sheets.

item.title: Trafigura Leads Race to Move US Fuel In-Country on Foreign Ships
item.description: Trafigura Group and Phillips 66 are the leading recipients of US government waivers allowing the use of foreign-flagged tankers to ship oil, fuel and other cargoes between domestic ports.

item.title: NY Plans Tax on City Homes Over $1 Million Bought With Cash
item.description: New York lawmakers are planning a new tax on New York City homes purchased in cash for at least $1 million, according to people familiar with the state budget negotiations.

item.title: Fed’s Miran to Step Down as Governor on Warsh Swearing In
item.description: Federal Reserve Governor Stephen Miran said he will resign from the central bank effective once incoming Chair Kevin Warsh is sworn into office, or shortly before.

item.title: Data Centers Drive 76% Rise in Power Bills on Largest US Grid
item.description: Power prices on the largest electric grid in the US jumped 76% in the first quarter due to rampant demand from data centers, adding to pressure on its operator to alleviate the strain on consumers.

item.title: UK Awards Permits to Dogger Bank South, North Fall Wind Farms
item.description: The UK government gave planning permission to three offshore wind farms, which could create as much as 4 gigawatts of new capacity as Britain steps up support for renewable energy.

item.title: Anti-Property Tax Push Spurs Downgrade Risk for Schools in Iowa
item.description: Certain Iowa school districts face a “one-in-two chance” of a credit rating downgrade by S&P Global Ratings after the state approved a new property tax reform measure.

item.title: ECB’s Stournaras Says High Oil Price Could Force Rate Hike, Report Says
item.description: Governing Council member Yannis Stournaras warned that the European Central Bank could be forced to hike borrowing costs if the price of oil maintains its current level, according to Athens News Agency.

item.title: Pension BCI Shuts Two Stock Funds, Cites Shrinking Public Pool
item.description: British Columbia Investment Management Corp. is closing two global stock-picking strategies that oversee about C$4.3 billion ($3.1 billion), as it contends with a contracting pool of publicly listed firms.

item.title: Spirit’s Collapse Shows Limits of Budget Model, Delta CEO Says
item.description: Delta Air Lines Chief Executive Officer Ed Bastian said rising jet fuel costs and Spirit Airlines’s collapse are accelerating a divide in the industry between carriers catering to premium travelers and those competing primarily on price.

item.title: Nvidia Gains 20% in Seven Days, Nearing $6 Trillion Market Value
item.description: Nvidia Corp. shares rose on Thursday, extending a 20% rally over the past seven days as investors plow into the chipmakers profiting from a flood of investment spending on artificial intelligence.

item.title: DraftKings Gets Rare Sell Rating as Kalshi, Polymarket Threaten Growth
item.description: The rapid expansion of sports prediction markets is hurting DraftKings Inc., according to the stock’s only bear on Wall Street.

item.title: Long-Stalled Crypto Bill Wins Key Senate Committee Vote
item.description: The Senate Banking Committee advanced a landmark digital asset market structure bill Thursday after months of negotiations, signaling fresh momentum for the long-stalled measure.

item.title: Pound Falls to One-Month Low on Burnham Run for Parliament
item.description: The pound fell to a one-month low after Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham said he is seeking to run for Parliament, potentially opening an avenue to challenge embattled UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer.

item.title: BOE’s Pill Says Strong Iran Price Pressures Warrant Rate Rise
item.description: The Bank of England should raise interest rates to fight off strong inflation spillovers from the Iran energy shock, according to its chief economist who set out his case for active policy in an unpredictable world.

item.title: Apple-OpenAI Alliance Frays, Setting Up Possible Legal Fight
item.description: Apple Inc.’s two-year-old partnership with OpenAI has become strained, according to people familiar with the matter, with the AI startup failing to see the expected benefits from the deal and now preparing possible legal action.

item.title: Pentagon Halts Plans to Deploy 4,000 Army Troops to Poland
item.description: The Pentagon scrapped plans to send about 4,000 Army troops to Poland, people familiar with the matter said, part of a broader review of the US military presence in Europe as President Donald Trump feuds with Germany and other members of the NATO alliance.

item.title: US Mortgage Rates Are Little Changed Despite Surging Inflation
item.description: Mortgage rates in the US were little changed even as the escalating energy crisis drives inflation higher.

item.title: Dollar’s Link to Oil Most Positive Ever as Iran Crisis Drags On
item.description: Eleven weeks into a Middle Eastern conflict that has shuttered a critical shipping channel and sent energy prices soaring, the US dollar’s linkage to oil prices is more positive than ever before.

item.title: Cerebras Shares Indicated to Surge 89% After Year’s Top IPO
item.description: Cerebras Systems Inc. shares are indicated to open 89% above their listing price, after the artificial intelligence chipmaker raised $5.55 billion in an upsized and hotly anticipated IPO.
